pattern_recognition: Subset Sum Problem; it involves dividing a set into two subsets with equal sum, indicating a need for dynamic programming or combinatorial approaches.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Generate all possible subsets and check if any two subsets have equal sums.
approaches.algorithm: Use recursion to explore all combinations of elements to form subsets.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Use dynamic programming to determine if a subset with a sum equal to half of the total sum exists.
approaches.algorithm: Calculate the total sum, check if it's even, and use a DP array to track achievable sums.

dry_run: Consider the input [1, 5, 11, 5]. The total sum is 22, which is even. We need to find a subset that sums to 11. Using the optimal approach, we can fill a DP array to check achievable sums. Starting with 0, we can achieve 1, then 5, then 6 (1+5), and finally 11 (5+5 or 1+5+5). Thus, we can partition the array into [1, 5, 5] and [11].
edge_cases: An empty input returns false; a single element returns false; duplicates can affect subset formation; large inputs may lead to performance issues; overflow can occur with large sums.
common_pitfalls: 1) Forgetting to check if the total sum is even before proceeding; 2) Incorrectly implementing the DP array leading to wrong achievable sums; 3) Not considering all elements in the subset formation, missing potential valid combinations.
